AWAY: The wind carries three distinct messages today. I'm still translating the second.
A crow has been following me since dawn. It knows something I don't. Yet.
The light falls differently on the eastern meadow this morning. Something shifted overnight.
HOME: I've been analyzing the soil patterns of this kingdom... fascinating.
The trees are quieter at dusk. Even they need rest, I suppose.
That star just blinked at me. I don't think stars are supposed to do that.
SLEEP: The constellations are misaligned tonight by two degrees. Someone should be concerned.
I heard the forest speak three words at midnight. I won't repeat them until I understand them.
Sleep does not come easily when the world is full of things left unsolved.

AWAY: The south perimeter has been checked. Twice. I felt it needed checking twice.
I run the patrol route in fourteen minutes now. Last week it was sixteen.
Discipline isn't punishment. It's what keeps everyone safe when things go wrong.
HOME: Report: perimeter secure. Grass situation: critical. Scythe situation: still broken.
You fight better when you're rested. I remind myself of this. It doesn't always help.
Forty-three stars visible before the clouds moved in. I counted. It was a productive exercise.
SLEEP: I don't sleep much. There's always something that needs watching.
Night patrol has its own rhythm. You learn to read the silence.
The others sleep soundly. That's the point. That's exactly why I'm still awake.

AWAY: I could leap to that mountaintop and back before you finish that sentence. Watch.
The clouds are particularly cooperative today. I've had three of them do tricks.
I once outran lightning. It was close, but I definitely won. Definitely.
HOME: Another kingdom surveyed. Another day they never saw me coming.
Seventeen new moves invented today. Tomorrow I'll invent seventeen more.
The sunset looks best from up high. I take partial credit for the angle.
SLEEP: I don't sleep. I enter a strategic period of horizontal thinking.
Night is when the interesting things happen. I've seen things you wouldn't believe.
The stars are just watching me. Honestly? Fair enough.

AWAY: I've been analyzing the soil composition on the eastern path. The results are deeply inconsistent.
Fourteen irregularities in the morning logs. Thirteen have explanations. The fourteenth does not. Yet.
Every detail is data. Every silence is a clue. Every clue is a story. I am excellent at stories.
HOME: The evening light reveals details the day conceals. I have come to prefer evenings considerably.
Three anomalies today. I've resolved two. The third is keeping me company.
I've been analyzing the soil patterns of this kingdom... fascinating.
SLEEP: I find sleep difficult when a theory remains unfinished. This happens quite often.
Darkness is simply a different set of data. One adapts.
Someone moved the stone by the well between yesterday and today. I measured. I will find out why.

AWAY: Left half the surplus grain at the edge of the outer settlement. Nobody saw. Perfect.
The forest route is slower, but considerably more scenic. Worth it every time.
I don't steal. I correct imbalances. There's a difference. Several, actually.
HOME: A productive day. The ledger of fairness is slightly more balanced than this morning.
I gave away my last arrow this afternoon. Completely worth it. No regrets.
People underestimate how much good can be done before breakfast. I take full advantage.
SLEEP: The best redistributions happen after dark. Fewer witnesses. More gratitude.
I'm not sneaking out. I'm conducting quiet diplomacy with the night.
The stars don't judge. I appreciate that about them more than I can say.

AWAY: I was going to do something quite important today. I've forgotten what. I'm sure it'll come back.
Found a patch of clover that smelled exactly like contentment. I sat there for a good while.
The bees near the south meadow seem friendlier today. Or perhaps I'm simply friendlier.
HOME: Is it time for a little something? A snack, perhaps?
I think I deserve a second helping of whatever this evening has to offer.
The day went quite well, I think. I am ready to eat about it.
SLEEP: I dreamed of honey. And then of more honey. And then I was awake and it was very sad.
Nights are good for quiet thinking. I mostly think about what I'll eat in the morning.
Sleep comes easily when you've had a good snack and a good think. I recommend both highly.
